summary: A sample generated pack showing how the six-agent pipeline turns an AI update into a complete multi-platform content sprint.
audience: Founders, operators, and growth teams building practical AI workflows
theme: Create content from the latest AI update

agent_log.summary: Selected a practical operator angle with a clear workflow promise.
Converted the update into reader-facing implications.
Rejected broad hype and preserved the specific implementation use case.
Drafted the core narrative and implementation path.
Adapted the sprint into platform-native formats.
Passed final clarity, specificity, voice, and CTA checks.

long_post.title: The AI update only matters if it changes the workflow
long_post.body_markdown:
**The mistake most teams make with AI news:** they share the announcement instead of translating it into a workflow.

The reader does not need another summary. They need to know what changed, why it matters, and what to try next.

Here is the sprint structure we use:

1. Identify the exact capability that changed.
2. Name the old workflow it replaces.
3. Show the new workflow in three practical steps.
4. Add the risk or limitation so the post feels credible.
5. End with a clear implementation CTA.

That is the difference between AI commentary and useful content.

The best content does not say, “this tool is powerful.” It says, “here is the task this now makes easier.”

x_thread.hook: Most AI updates are not content. They are raw material. The job is to turn the update into a workflow your audience can use.
tweets: 1/ The announcement is rarely the angle. The workflow change is the angle.
2/ Ask: what task becomes faster, cheaper, or more reliable because of this update?
3/ Then map the before and after. Before: manual research, blank-page writing, scattered notes. After: one prompt, one review pass, one publishable asset.
4/ Add the limitation. Good content gets more credible when it says what the tool still cannot do.
5/ Package the result into a post, thread, caption, carousel, script, and LinkedIn version. One insight. Six distribution surfaces.

ig_caption.hook: Stop posting AI announcements. Translate them.
ig_caption.body:
Your audience does not need another generic update.

They need to know what changed in their actual workflow.

The simple filter:

What got faster?
What got cheaper?
What got easier to delegate?
What still needs human review?

Answer those four questions and the update becomes useful content.
ig_caption.cta: Save this as your AI-news content filter.

slides.title: AI News Is Not the Content
bullets: The workflow change is the angle

short_script.title: Turn AI Updates Into Workflow Content
beats: HOOK: Most AI updates are not worth posting until you translate them.
PROBLEM: Everyone repeats the same announcement, so the content sounds identical.
SHIFT: Ask what workflow actually changed for the reader.
EXAMPLE: If research got faster, show the old research loop and the new one.
QUALITY: Add the limitation so the content feels credible.
CTA: Use one update to create a post, thread, caption, carousel, script, and LinkedIn version.
